recipes.title: Mustard and Lemon Dressing
recipes.summary: A bright everyday dressing that works with salads, fish and warm vegetables.
method: Whisk until emulsified.
Add a teaspoon of water if a thinner dressing is needed.
recipes.note: English mustard variation: Use only 1/2 teaspoon English mustard and include honey to soften the heat.

recipes.title: Zucchini, Caramelised Onion and Emmental Frittata
recipes.summary: Caramelised onion, tender courgette and enough cheese to make added salt optional.
method: Heat the oil over medium heat.
Cook the onion for four minutes.
Add the honey and continue cooking gently.
Add a tablespoon of water whenever the pan becomes too dry.
Add the courgette and cook until tender but not mushy.
Beat the eggs with black pepper and most of the Emmental.
Pour into the pan and lower the heat.
Cover and cook until nearly set.
Turn briefly, or finish under a grill only if the pan is oven-safe.
recipes.note: Good hot and cold. The caramelised onion and cheese make added salt largely unnecessary. Water works better than extra oil when the onion starts catching.

recipes.title: Air-Fryer Salmon with Vegetables
recipes.summary: A simple working-day dinner with crisp vegetables and properly juicy salmon.
ingredients: Up to 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil for the full dish
method: Cut vegetables into fairly thick pieces.
Start the vegetables first at 190 C.
Add the salmon later so it cooks for only eight to ten minutes.
Remove the salmon as soon as it is just cooked; residual heat will continue the process.
Finish with lemon and black pepper.
recipes.note: Thin courgette slices overcook quickly. The salmon was best removed slightly before the timer ended.

recipes.title: Bresaola, Rocket and Parmesan Plate
recipes.summary: Minimal preparation, strong flavours and a sensible portion of bread on the side.
method: Arrange the rocket and bresaola.
Add shaved Parmesan and tomatoes.
Finish with oil, lemon and black pepper.
Serve with wholemeal bread.

recipes.title: Dijon, Honey and Lemon Tofu
recipes.summary: A sharp-sweet marinade that gives super-firm tofu real flavour without drying it out.
method: Cut the tofu into two-centimetre cubes.
Mix the marinade ingredients.
Coat the tofu and leave for 15-20 minutes.
Air fry at 190 C for eight minutes.
Check immediately; super-firm tofu dries quickly.
Add only one or two further minutes if needed.
recipes.note: M&S super-firm tofu was already dry at nine minutes. Use smooth Dijon, not wholegrain, for a proper coating.

recipes.title: Moroccan-Style Chickpea and Harissa Couscous
recipes.summary: Warm, spicy couscous balanced with lemon, parsley and Greek yoghurt.
ingredients: 80 g dry couscous, prepared and fluffed
method: Cook the onion in one teaspoon of oil.
Add small splashes of water to prevent burning.
Add honey after about four minutes.
Add garlic for 30 seconds.
Add chickpeas and cook for two or three minutes.
Stir in the harissa and a little water.
Add tomatoes briefly, if using.
Fold in the couscous.
Finish with lemon, parsley and black pepper.
Serve with Greek yoghurt on top.
recipes.note: The yoghurt balances the harissa. Do not rely on oil alone to cook the onion; water prevents scorching.

recipes.title: Tuna and Mustard Salad
recipes.summary: A fast lunch with protein, acidity and crunch, built from whatever is actually in the fridge.
ingredients: Thinly sliced onion or spring onion
method: Combine the salad ingredients.
Whisk the oil, lemon, mustard, honey and pepper.
Dress immediately before eating.

recipes.title: Avocado and Egg on Toast
recipes.summary: Wholemeal toast, half an avocado and one properly cooked egg.
method: Toast the bread.
Mash the avocado with the lemon juice and pepper.
Poach or fry the egg with minimal oil.
Assemble and finish with chilli flakes.
recipes.note: Half an avocado is the portion; the other half keeps with its stone and lemon. The lemon is not optional; it lifts the whole thing.

recipes.title: Couscous with Salmon, Lemon and Herbs
recipes.summary: Air-fryer salmon flaked through bright, herby couscous.
ingredients: 80 g dry couscous, prepared and fluffed
method: Air fry the salmon at 190 C for eight to ten minutes.
Prepare the couscous with the lemon zest.
Flake the salmon into the couscous.
Add the spring onion, herbs, oil and lemon juice.
Finish with black pepper.
recipes.note: Same salmon timing as the air-fryer salmon recipe: remove it slightly early. The zest goes in the couscous water; the juice goes in at the end.

recipes.title: Eggs in Tomato Sauce
recipes.summary: Three eggs gently cooked in a reduced tomato sauce, with bread for the inevitable scarpetta.
method: Soften the onion in oil, adding water if needed.
Add garlic for 30 seconds.
Add passata, chilli, pepper and oregano.
Simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Make three wells and crack in the eggs.
Cover and cook for five to seven minutes.
Serve with bread.

recipes.title: French Onion Soup with Gruyère
recipes.summary: Slow caramelised onions using the water-splash technique, topped with a Gruyère toast.
method: Cook the onions slowly in the oil for 20-25 minutes.
Add small splashes of water whenever they start to catch.
Add the honey halfway through.
Deglaze with the wine, if using.
Add the stock and thyme; simmer for 15 minutes.
Toast the bread, top with Gruyère and grill until melted.
Serve the soup with the toast on top and black pepper.
recipes.note: Same onion technique as the frittata, just scaled up and slower. The onions decide the whole soup; do not rush the first stage.

recipes.title: Greek Yoghurt with Oats, Honey and Fruit
recipes.summary: A five-minute breakfast with protein, slow carbs and just enough honey.
ingredients: A handful of berries or half a banana
method: Stir the oats into the yoghurt.
Rest for five minutes, or overnight in the fridge.
Top with the fruit, honey and cinnamon.
recipes.note: Overnight, the oats soften completely and it eats like a dessert. Measure the honey; it is the easiest place for sugar to creep.

recipes.title: Harissa Shakshuka
recipes.summary: The eggs-in-tomato formula pushed towards North Africa: harissa, cumin, red pepper and cooling yoghurt.
method: Soften the onion and pepper in the oil, with splashes of water.
Add the garlic for 30 seconds, then harissa and cumin.
Add the passata and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Make three wells and crack in the eggs.
Cover and cook for five to seven minutes.
Top with yoghurt and parsley; serve with the bread.
recipes.note: The yoghurt balances the harissa, as in the couscous. Same base as eggs in tomato sauce; the spices do the travelling.

recipes.title: Mushroom Risotto
recipes.summary: A proper risotto finished with Parmesan and a spoon of stock instead of butter.
method: Brown the mushrooms first and set them aside.
Soften the onion in the oil, adding splashes of water as needed.
Add the garlic for 30 seconds, then the rice for one minute.
Add the hot stock a ladle at a time, stirring often.
Return the mushrooms for the last five minutes.
Off the heat, beat in the Parmesan with a final spoon of stock.
Rest for two minutes, then finish with parsley and pepper.
recipes.note: The final beating with Parmesan and stock replaces the usual butter. The stock must be hot; cold stock stalls the cooking.

recipes.title: Mushroom and Gruyère Frittata
recipes.summary: The frittata formula with browned mushrooms, thyme and Gruyère.
method: Brown the mushrooms in the oil and set aside.
Soften the onion in the same pan, using splashes of water.
Return the mushrooms with the thyme.
Beat the eggs with pepper and most of the Gruyère.
Pour in, lower the heat, cover and cook until nearly set.
Flip briefly, or grill only if the pan is oven-safe.
recipes.note: Same structure as the courgette frittata; only the filling changes. Brown the mushrooms properly first or they will water down the eggs.

recipes.title: Pasta with Courgette and Lemon
recipes.summary: Courgette cooked down until almost creamy, lifted with lemon and Parmesan.
ingredients: 1 large courgette, half thinly sliced, half grated
method: Cook the pasta; keep a cup of the water.
Cook the sliced courgette in the oil until golden.
Add the grated courgette and garlic; cook until soft and almost creamy.
Add the pasta with a splash of its water and the lemon zest.
Off the heat, add the Parmesan and lemon juice.
Finish with pepper and basil.
recipes.note: The grated half melts into a sauce; the sliced half keeps texture. Lemon juice off the heat only, or it turns bitter.

recipes.title: Pasta with Mushrooms and Gruyère
recipes.summary: Browned chestnut mushrooms, a silky pasta-water sauce and Gruyère added off the heat.
method: Cook the pasta; keep a cup of the water.
Brown the mushrooms in the oil over high heat without crowding the pan.
Add the garlic for 30 seconds.
Add the drained pasta with a splash of pasta water and toss.
Off the heat, stir in the Gruyère until it melts into the sauce.
Finish with parsley and black pepper.
recipes.note: Gruyère goes stringy if boiled; always add it off the heat. Mushrooms brown properly only if the pan is not crowded.
